title: WMG AQA Essentials — Assessment, Rubrics, MA1 & CA1

questions.q: When are assessments due to be submitted to AQ for the 26/27 assessment approval cycle?
What must assessment rubrics contain to support faster and easier feedback?
answers: A single generic mark band description used across all modules
Learning outcome-specific wording, adapting the standard wording as appropriate
Marks allocated separately for presentation, spelling, punctuation and grammar
The staff-facing marking guidance copied into each row

questions.q: True or false: an assessment can stay the same from one academic year to the next.
answers: True — provided the learning outcomes have not changed
True — provided the external examiner approves no change
False — assessments must be substantially different to last year and changed every academic year
False — but only the resit assessment needs to change

questions.q: By what date must New Course Approvals or updates to courses (CA1/MA1 paperwork) for 28/29 launch be submitted?
The MA1 updates being submitted in the next academic year are for which launch year?
How many times can an assessment normally be used within the same academic year before it must be replaced?
answers: Unlimited, provided the rubric is updated

questions.q: Which document is student-facing and must be provided alongside the assessment brief?
Within how many University working days must marks and feedback normally be returned to students?
